drop_reason: Three-way mismatch between task name, verifier, and canonical patch. (1) Task name + verifier F2P1-4 (weights 0.60: F2P1=0.30 'no ïnf typo' + F2P2=0.20 captions alignment + F2P3=0.30 no malformed float literals + F2P4=0.20 all float literals evaluate) targets the `float('ïnf')` Unicode-typo fix in `library/train_util.py`. (2) Canonical patch at data-pipeline/artifacts_sd-scripts/canonical_patches/ses_390b1cf89ffevwBOm1vuhP83gF.json (43 additions, 0 deletions, lossy reconstruction with 7 hunk-anchor warnings) consists ONLY of two new methods on DreamBoothDataset — `rebalance_regularization_images()` and `filter_registered_images_by_orig_resolution()` — and does NOT contain the float typo fix. (3) Upstream gates (test.sh:221-267) check for `skip_image_resolution` parameter on BaseDataset.__init__ (0.20 weight, never appears in the canonical session — session uses `min_orig_resolution`/`max_orig_resolution`) and `resize_image` arg-order with shape indices (0.20 weight, vacuously passes when no shape-based call exists). The session's user simulation (5 turns: avoid get_image_size, why None sizes, move filtering to bucket-prep, minimize diff, captions audit) covers a real conversation but the verifier scoring is not aligned to either the canonical patch (rebalance methods) or the discussed multi-turn improvements. The `f2p_upstream_skip_resolution` gate's claim that BaseDataset should accept `skip_image_resolution` is fabricated — no such parameter exists in the session diff or canonical patch.
notes: The original session (ses_390b1cf89ffevwBOm1vuhP83gF) is a code review where the user asks the agent to find bugs in staged changes adding `min_orig_resolution`/`max_orig_resolution` resolution filtering. The agent identifies the `float('ïnf')` typo at train_util.py:2416 and the captions-list misalignment after filtering. So the verifier's F2P1-4 *do* match what the user found in the session — but they do NOT match the canonical patch reconstruction stored in artifacts_sd-scripts/canonical_patches/. The upstream wrapper gates (added by the v5 orchestrator on top of the inner verifier) are testing for the wrong feature entirely (`skip_image_resolution` does not exist in this session's universe — it appears 0 times). Even if we accept the inner block as session-faithful (typo + captions), the upstream gates contribute 0.40 of the total weight and test fabricated APIs, capping the realistic max score around 0.80 for a perfect typo+caption fix and silently giving free 0.20 from the vacuous resize_image check. Sister tasks `sd-scripts-multiresolution-compat`, `sd-scripts-skip-resolution-tuple`, `sd-scripts-reg-image-dedup`, and `sd-scripts-sdxl-multires-dedup` cover the resolution-filtering feature space coherently from other sd-scripts sessions; this task is the malformed duplicate. (Existing skip-record at this path was 'no e2b template' — replaced with curator verdict per task spec.)
